steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Use the radians-to-degrees conversion
steps.content: To convert radians to degrees, multiply by $$\frac{180^\circ}{\pi}$$

steps.title: Apply it to the given measure
steps.content: The given difference is $-\frac{5}{12}\pi$ radians, so the degree expression is $$-\frac{5}{12}\pi\cdot\frac{180^\circ}{\pi}$$

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content: That expression matches C. The factor with $\pi$ in the denominator cancels the radians unit.
